Title: CIN-like TCP13 is essential for plant growth regulation under dehydration stress

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: TCP13 is one of the CIN-like TCP genes that lacks the miR319A/JAW target sequence and is more similar in sequence to TCP5 and TCP17 than to other class II TCPs. Its promoter uniquely contains ABRE motifs (ACGTGG), whereas TCP5 and TCP17 promoters harbor GMSAUR auxin-responsive elements, consistent with a distinct ABA/dehydration-inducible role for TCP13. Functional redundancy with TCP5 and TCP17 is evident for leaf growth under osmotic stress in tcp5/13/17 triple mutants, but inhibition of root growth and dehydration tolerance appear to be unique functions of TCP13 among CIN-like TCPs.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-plant transcriptome (Agilent Arabidopsis 4 Oligo Microarray, 21,500 probes) comparing TCP13pro::TCP13SRDX plants and controls; 555 genes significantly downregulated (FC < 0.5, p < 0.05, FDR < 0.0277). Data deposited in ArrayExpress under accession E-MTAB-9336.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Key positive regulator of dehydration stress tolerance that modulates leaf and root growth: overexpression causes narrow, downward-rolled leaves, reduced leaf and primary root growth, ABA-hypersensitive root growth inhibition, hypersensitivity of leaf growth to osmotic stress, and enhanced survival with reduced water loss under severe dehydration; loss-of-function (tcp13) leads to ABA-insensitive root growth and reduced induction of dehydration- and ABA-inducible genes.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Nuclear CIN-like TCP transcription factor whose dehydration-inducible expression is associated with ABRE motifs in its promoter. By binding TCP motifs in target promoters, it acts mainly as a transcriptional activator: SRDX-fused TCP13 represses genes containing TCP binding motifs, and many targets also contain ABREs. TCP13 is required for full activation of ABA-regulated genes (AHG3, Gols2, RD20, NCED3) in response to ABA/dehydration and for auxin-regulated genes (IAA5, LBD1, IAA19, PIN4) in response to IAA/dehydration, thereby modulating leaf and root growth under stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in ABA- and dehydration-responsive transcriptional networks and auxin signaling under dehydration and osmotic stress. Acts downstream of ABA signaling to promote inhibition of primary root growth and positively regulates dehydration- and ABA-inducible genes (e.g., AHG3, RD20, NCED3, Gols2) and auxin-responsive genes (e.g., IAA5, IAA19, LBD1, PIN4), integrating ABA and auxin pathways in the dehydration stress response.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Regulates ABA and auxin signaling pathways and downstream genes involved in raffinose, proline, ABA metabolism, jasmonic acid metabolism, and flavonoid biosynthesis.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation:
TCP13 --(activation)--> ABA- and dehydration-inducible genes HAI1, HAI2, AHG3, RD20, RD26, RD29B, GolS2, P5CS1, and NCED3 (downregulated in TCP13pro::TCP13SRDX plants; reduced induction in tcp13 mutants under dehydration and in response to ABA; AHG3 and Gols2 promoter-LUC activation reduced in tcp13 protoplasts with ABA).
TCP13 --(activation)--> auxin signaling genes IAA1, IAA5, IAA14, IAA17, IAA19, IAA34, SAUR17, SAUR34, PIN4, and LBD1 (downregulated in TCP13pro::TCP13SRDX plants; dehydration-inducible expression of IAA5, IAA19, LBD1 and basal PIN4 reduced in tcp13 mutants; IAA5 and LBD1 promoter-LUC activation reduced in tcp13 protoplasts with IAA).
TCP13 --(activation)--> flavonoid biosynthesis genes MYB12, MYB111, FLS1, FLS2, FLS4, CHI/TT5, CHIL, F3H/TT6, UGT73B2, and UGT78D1 (downregulated in TCP13pro::TCP13SRDX plants).
TCP13 expression --(induction)--> by dehydration stress and weakly by ABA and salt (via ABRE motifs in its promoter).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CIN-like class II TCP transcription factor of the TEOSINTE BRANCHED1/CYCLOIDEA/PCF (TCP) family, containing a conserved noncanonical bHLH domain.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, based on TCP13-sGFP fusion protein transiently expressed in Arabidopsis mesophyll protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Strongly expressed in leaves (cotyledons and true leaves), especially in expanding cells at the upper region of young leaves; low expression in roots. Induced early and strongly by dehydration stress and slightly by ABA and high salinity. Public expression data indicate expression in leaf mesophyll cells and in root endodermis and cortex under salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dehydration stress (leaf desiccation on parafilm and withholding water from soil-grown plants), osmotic stress with 25 mM mannitol, ABA treatment (5–100 μM), and salt stress with 175 mM NaCl.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Dehydration and osmotic stress tolerance; ABA and auxin signaling; regulation of leaf morphology and root growth by a CIN-like TCP transcription factor.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress response (dehydration/drought, osmotic stress, ABA sensitivity) and plant growth traits (leaf morphology, leaf growth, root growth).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In microarray analysis, 555 genes were downregulated in TCP13pro::TCP13SRDX plants (fold-change < 0.5; p < 0.05; FDR < 0.0277), of which 230 were dehydration-inducible genes. In dehydration tolerance tests, 90 WT and 45 plants each of 35Spro::TCP13OXa and 35Spro::TCP13OXb were assayed, and 35Spro::TCP13OX plants showed significantly higher survival rates and significantly lower water loss from detached leaves than WT (P values ranging from <0.05 to <0.001). ABA and mannitol treatments caused significantly stronger inhibition of root elongation and leaf area, respectively, in 35Spro::TCP13OX plants and weaker inhibition in tcp13 mutants compared with WT (P < 0.05–0.001).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Together with TCP5 and TCP17, contributes to normal leaf size and morphology: tcp5/13/17 triple mutants have smaller leaves with shorter petioles and slightly wavy, serrated margins. TCP13 repression via TCP13pro::TCP13SRDX increases root growth and causes wavy, serrated rosette leaves.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Columbia (Col-0; WT); T-DNA insertion mutants tcp13 (GK_182B12) and tcp5/13/17 triple mutant; overexpression lines 35Spro::TCP13OX; chimeric repressor lines 35Spro::TCP13SRDX and TCP13pro::TCP13SRDX; reporter lines TCP13pro::GUS and 35Spro::TCP13-sGFP; protoplasts isolated from WT and tcp13 rosette leaves.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Abiotic stress treatments (dehydration on parafilm, soil dehydration by withholding water, salt stress with NaCl, osmotic stress with mannitol, ABA treatment); transgenic overexpression and SRDX-based chimeric repression; phenotyping of leaf morphology, leaf area, hypocotyl and root length; histochemical GUS staining for promoter activity; qRT-PCR gene expression analysis; whole-plant microarray transcriptome analysis; motif enrichment analysis in promoters; GFP fusion and confocal microscopy for subcellular localization; dual-luciferase protoplast reporter assays for AHG3, Gols2, IAA5, and LBD1 promoters.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a dehydration-inducible CIN-like TCP gene from Arabidopsis leaf microarray data; functional analysis by reverse genetics including overexpression (35Spro::TCP13OX), chimeric repressor (35Spro::TCP13SRDX, TCP13pro::TCP13SRDX), knockout mutant tcp13, and tcp5/13/17 triple mutant.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: TCP13 is a dehydration-inducible regulator whose overexpression restricts leaf expansion, promotes leaf rolling, and inhibits root growth while enhancing dehydration tolerance and reducing water loss, suggesting that targeted modulation of TCP13 activity could be used to improve drought/dehydration tolerance through controlled adjustment of shoot and root growth. However, strong constitutive activation incurs growth penalties, so practical applications would likely require stress- or tissue-specific tuning of TCP13 expression in crops.
